forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8280'
+project: ai-panel
+title: Verify the ACP agent honors newSession.mcpServers
+---
+## What
+The TypeScript ACP client gives the agent the kanban tool by putting a stdio MCP server entry in the ACP `newSession` request's `mcpServers` array. Confirm the Rust ACP agent honors that field per session and connects the server.
+
+- `claude-agent` has `convert_acp_to_internal_mcp_config` (`crates/claude-agent/src/agent.rs` ~line 1200/1408) â€” verify it actually connects the `mcpServers` from a `newSession` request and exposes the tools.
+- Verify the llama-agent ACP path (`crates/llama-agent/src/acp/`) reads and connects `newSession.mcpServers` likewise.
+- If either backend ignores the field, fix it in the agent crate. The kanban-app must not work around a gap.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Task comments record whether `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` honor `newSession.mcpServers`.
+- [ ] If a gap exists it is fixed in the agent crate so a session created with `mcpServers` connects them.
+- [ ] A session created with one MCP server in `mcpServers` exposes that server's tools to the agent.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test (`crates/claude-agent/tests/` or `crates/acp-conformance`): start a test MCP server, create a session passing it in `newSession.mcpServers`, assert the agent can list/call that server's tool.
+- [ ] Equivalent coverage for the llama-agent path if a gap is found there.
+- [ ] `cargo test` for the affected crate(s) is green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the failing `mcpServers`-at-`newSession` integration test first.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,24 +4,24 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8280'
 project: ai-panel
-title: Verify the ACP agent honors newSession.mcpServers
+title: Verify the ACP agent honors an HTTP MCP server in newSession.mcpServers
 ---
 ## What
-The TypeScript ACP client gives the agent the kanban tool by putting a stdio MCP server entry in the ACP `newSession` request's `mcpServers` array. Confirm the Rust ACP agent honors that field per session and connects the server.
+The TypeScript ACP client gives the agent the full SwissArmyHammer toolset by putting an HTTP MCP server entry â€” `McpServer::Http` pointing at the board's in-process SAH toolset URL â€” in the ACP `newSession` request's `mcpServers` array. Confirm the Rust ACP agent honors HTTP `mcpServers` entries per session and connects them.

-- `claude-agent` has `convert_acp_to_internal_mcp_config` (`crates/claude-agent/src/agent.rs` ~line 1200/1408) â€” verify it actually connects the `mcpServers` from a `newSession` request and exposes the tools.
-- Verify the llama-agent ACP path (`crates/llama-agent/src/acp/`) reads and connects `newSession.mcpServers` likewise.
+- `claude-agent` has `convert_acp_to_internal_mcp_config` (`crates/claude-agent/src/agent.rs`) and uses `claude_agent::config::McpServerConfig::Http` â€” verify it actually connects an HTTP `mcpServers` entry from `newSession` and exposes its tools.
+- Verify the llama-agent ACP path (`crates/llama-agent/src/acp/`) reads and connects HTTP `mcpServers` likewise.
 - If either backend ignores the field, fix it in the agent crate. The kanban-app must not work around a gap.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Task comments record whether `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` honor `newSession.mcpServers`.
-- [ ] If a gap exists it is fixed in the agent crate so a session created with `mcpServers` connects them.
-- [ ] A session created with one MCP server in `mcpServers` exposes that server's tools to the agent.
+- [ ] Task comments record whether `claude-agent` and `llama-agent` honor an HTTP entry in `newSession.mcpServers`.
+- [ ] If a gap exists it is fixed in the agent crate so a session created with an HTTP `mcpServers` entry connects it.
+- [ ] A session created with one HTTP MCP server in `mcpServers` exposes that server's tools to the agent.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test (`crates/claude-agent/tests/` or `crates/acp-conformance`): start a test MCP server, create a session passing it in `newSession.mcpServers`, assert the agent can list/call that server's tool.
+- [ ] Integration test (`crates/claude-agent/tests/` or `crates/acp-conformance`): start a test HTTP MCP server, create a session passing it as an `McpServer::Http` in `newSession.mcpServers`, assert the agent can list/call that server's tool.
 - [ ] Equivalent coverage for the llama-agent path if a gap is found there.
 - [ ] `cargo test` for the affected crate(s) is green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` â€” write the failing `mcpServers`-at-`newSession` integration test first.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the failing HTTP-`mcpServers`-at-`newSession` integration test first.
\ No newline at end of file
